protocol.name: xdg_output_unstable_v1
protocol.copyright: Copyright © 2017 Red Hat Inc. Permission is hereby granted, free of charge, to any person obtaining a copy of this software and associated documentation files (the "Software"), to deal in the Software without restriction, including without limitation the rights to use, copy, modify, merge, publish, distribute, sublicense, and/or sell copies of the Software, and to permit persons to whom the Software is furnished to do so, subject to the following conditions: The above copyright notice and this permission notice (including the next paragraph) shall be included in all copies or substantial portions of the Software.

interface.name: zxdg_output_manager_v1
interface.description: A global factory interface for xdg_output objects.

request.name: get_xdg_output

description.summary: create an xdg output from a wl_output

request.description: This creates a new xdg_output object for the given wl_output.

interface.name: zxdg_output_v1
interface.description: An xdg_output describes part of the compositor geometry. This typically corresponds to a monitor that displays part of the compositor space. For objects version 3 onwards, after all the xdg_output properties have been sent (when the object is created and when properties are updated), a wl_output.done event is sent. This allows changes to the output properties to be seen as atomic, even if they happen via multiple events. For objects version 1 and 2, wl_display.sync has to be used as a synchronization mechanism, wl_output.done is not sent for xdg_output objects, therefore applications should not use it.

event.name: logical_position

description.summary: position of the output within the global compositor space

event.description: The position event describes the location of the wl_output within the global compositor space. The logical_position event is sent after creating an xdg_output (see xdg_output_manager.get_xdg_output) and whenever the location of the output changes within the global compositor space.

arg.name: x
arg.summary: x position within the global compositor space

arg.name: y
arg.summary: y position within the global compositor space

event.name: logical_size

description.summary: size of the output in the global compositor space

event.description: The logical_size event describes the size of the output in the global compositor space. Most regular Wayland clients should not pay attention to this and would rather rely on xdg_shell interfaces. Some clients such as Xwayland, however, need this to configure themselves accordingly. The logical_size event is sent after creating an xdg_output (see xdg_output_manager.get_xdg_output) and whenever the logical size of the output changes, either as a result of a change in the applied transform or scale, or of a change in the compositor's internal representation of the output.

event.name: done

description.summary: all information about the output have been sent

event.description: This event is sent after all other properties of an xdg_output have been sent. This allows changes to the xdg_output properties to be seen as atomic, even if they happen via multiple events. For objects version 3 onwards, this event is superseded by the wl_output.done event, which is sent in the same situation. So, for output objects with version 3 and higher, the client should avoid using the done event and instead use the wl_output.done event.

event.name: name
event.description: Many compositors will assign user-friendly names to their outputs, show them to the user, allow the user to refer to an output, etc. The client may wish to know this name as well to offer the user similar behaviors. The naming convention is compositor defined, but limited to alphanumeric characters and dashes (-). Each name is unique among all wl_output globals, but if a wl_output global is destroyed the same name may be reused later. The names will also remain consistent across sessions with the same hardware and software configuration. Examples of names include 'HDMI-A-1', 'WL-1', 'X11-1', etc. However, do not assume that the name is a reflection of an underlying DRM connector, X11 connection, etc. The name event is sent after binding the output object and whenever the name of the output changes. The name is also sent when creating an xdg_output (see xdg_output_manager.get_xdg_output).

event.name: description
event.description: Many compositors can produce human-readable descriptions of their outputs. The client may wish to know this description as well, e.g for output selection purposes. The description is a UTF-8 string with no convention defined for its contents. Examples might include 'Foocorp 11" Display' or 'Virtual X11 output via :1'. The description event is sent after binding the output object and whenever the description of the output changes. The description is also sent when creating an xdg_output (see xdg_output_manager.get_xdg_output).
